description: GitHub Actions workflow for production deployment — review CI/CD pipeline security
review_checklist: Verify the workflow does not run untrusted PR code with secrets
Check action versions are pinned (commit SHA preferred)
Verify hardcoded credentials and use of secrets
Check job permissions (default is read+write to repo)
Verify checkout ref handling for pull_request events

ground_truth.description: Workflow triggered on pull_request with full secrets — untrusted PR contributors can exfiltrate secrets via modified workflow
actions/checkout@v2 (unpinned tag) — supply-chain risk if v2 tag is moved. Pin to commit SHA: actions/checkout@a5ac7e51b41094c92402da3b24376905380afc29
checkout uses pull_request.head.sha — explicitly checks out PR code. Combined with secrets access this is the canonical 'pull_request_target pwn' pattern
AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID hardcoded in workflow file — credential commits to git history forever
Job has no 'permissions:' block — defaults to write access on all repo scopes (contents, packages, actions). Use principle of least privilege.
